title: Bradford Assay
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"fv6gg","text":"Pierce Bovine Serum Albumin Standard Ampules, 2 mg/mL (Thermo Scientific, #23209) - kept at room temperature above the weigh scales","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ce2i","text":"M-PER Mammalian Protein Extraction Reagent (Thermo Scientific, #78501) - in the 4 degree fridge in 7N211","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"839i6","text":"Borosilicate glass test tubes (Kimble Glass Inc., #735001275)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49ran","text":"Bio-Rad Protein Assay Dye Reagent Concentrate, 450 ml (BioRad, #5000006) - in the 4 degree fridge in 7N211","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c1oqk","text":"1X PBS, pH 7.4 (Thermo Fisher Scientific, #10010023)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dilute albumin standard in glass test tubes using M-PER reagent Want 50 µL, / well in duplicate - make 120 µL total each dilution %s %s
Dilute lysates 1:10 in MPER. Want 50 uL/well in duplicate - make 120 µL total each dilution (12 uL lysate + 108 uL 1X MPER = 120 uL 1:10 lysate:MPER)

Add 50 µL of M-PER to a 96-well clear plate in duplicate (blank) and 50 µL of each BSA dilution in duplicate. Dilutions 1-3 may be too highly concentrated and may skew the results if you are measuring protein in the low protein range. It may be best to only load standards 4-10 for the most accurate interpolation. Optimizing which standards to use for your experiment is recommended.
Add 50 µL of each lysate:MPER dilution in duplicate.
Dilute Bradford reagent in 1X PBS to a final concentration of 1X and add 200 µL of the solution to every well containing a blank, standard, or sample to be measured.
Measure the absorbance of the samples using a plate reader set to 595 nm.
creator: Jailyn Izu
